short_video_script:
For two years I avoided posting videos. Not because I had nothing to say. Because I was afraid my ideas were not polished enough.

I once recorded a 90-second video for our product launch. Watched it back three times. Then deleted it.

I kept comparing myself to people with perfectly scripted content. Every time I decided: not yet.

Then a founder I respect posted a rough, unscripted video. It got ten times the engagement of their polished ones. The rawness was the point.

If you have been waiting to feel ready — you do not need to be polished. You need to be real.

selected_moments.source_text: I kept thinking: this is not good enough.
selected_moments.reason: Captures the inner critic — most viewers feel this but never say it aloud.

selected_moments.source_text: I recorded a 90-second video for our product launch... and deleted it.
selected_moments.reason: Concrete story — makes the fear tangible, not abstract.

selected_moments.source_text: A founder I respect posted a rough video and got ten times the engagement.
selected_moments.reason: The turning point — gives the audience a reason to act differently.

discarded_moments.source_text: I know I will hate how I sound.
discarded_moments.reason: Weaker than the 'not polished enough' fear — the more universal angle was used instead.
